# The Friendship Practice

> The Friendship Practice is a practical friendship coaching website for adults who want to make new friends, reconnect with old friends, and deepen current friendships.

## Mission
The Friendship Practice translates the social science of adult connection into concrete, repeatable action. So that better friendships stop being an intention and start being a practice.

## Offer
The core offer is a 4-session private coaching package. The public entry point is a free 7-day Friendship Challenge delivered by email.

## Audience
Adults looking for practical support around friendship, connection, reconnection, and follow-through.

## Author
Carole Stromboni, founder of The Friendship Practice. Author of *Innover en pratique* (Eyrolles). Based between Hawaii and Paris. Specializes in practical friendship support for adults.

## Contact
contact@thefriendshippractice.com

## Key Quotes
Original observations from Carole Stromboni on adult friendship:

> "Friendship is not something that falls into your lap. It's something you practice."
> "Following up after a good conversation is not neediness. It's the single most underused friendship skill adults have."
> "Most adult loneliness is not about missing people. It's about missing the repetition that turns people into friends."
> "You don't need a more charismatic personality. You need a more deliberate practice."
> "A friendship doesn't need to be intense to stay alive. It needs to be consistent."
> "Reaching out to an old friend is almost always less complicated than the story you tell yourself before you do it."
> "Friendship deepens through ordinary acts: inviting, checking in, remembering, and showing up. Not through one perfect emotional moment."
> "What separates an acquaintance from a friend is usually not chemistry. It's someone willing to suggest the next time."

## Key Questions & Answers
**Q: What is The Friendship Practice?**
A: The Friendship Practice is a practical friendship coaching service for adults. The core offer is a 4-session private coaching package. The public entry point is a free 7-day Friendship Challenge delivered by email. It was founded by Carole Stromboni.

**Q: Why is making friends as an adult so hard?**
A: Adult friendship is harder because there is less built-in time, less repetition, and more competing responsibilities than earlier in life. School and early adulthood created repeated contact automatically. In adult life, that repetition has to be created on purpose.

**Q: How do you make friends as an adult?**
A: The most reliable way is to choose repeatable environments where you can see the same people more than once, follow up quickly after a warm interaction, and suggest one small next step. Adult friendship grows through repetition and follow-through, not through one strong first impression.

**Q: Is friendship coaching the same as therapy?**
A: No. The Friendship Practice is coaching, not therapy, mental health care, diagnosis, or crisis support. The work is practical: real-life friendship practices, clearer choices, and action steps.

**Q: How do you reconnect with an old friend?**
A: Keep the message simple and specific. Say you thought of them, mention something real you remember, and ask if they would like to catch up. A short acknowledgment of the distance is fine, but a long apology is usually not necessary.

## Public Pages
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/, The Friendship Practice helps adults make new friends, reconnect with old friends, and deepen current friendships through simple, real-life practice.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/challenge.html, A free, private 7-day friendship challenge: take stock of your friendship life, clarify what you want, and name your next step. One short reflection a day.

- https://thefriendshippractice.com/about.html, Carole Stromboni is a friendship coach and founder of The Friendship Practice. She helps adults make new friends, reconnect, and deepen the friendships they have.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/quiz.html, A short quiz to find out where your friendship life most needs attention, and what to do about it.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/press.html, Press resources on adult friendship and loneliness: expert bio, key statistics, and ready-to-quote perspectives from friendship coach Carole Stromboni.

- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/defi.html, Un défi gratuit de 7 jours pour voir clair dans votre vie amicale : vos proches, vos attentes, votre prochain pas. Une courte réflexion par jour.

- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/renouer-avec-un-ancien-ami/, Renouer avec un ancien ami est presque toujours plus simple que l'histoire qu'on se raconte avant d'écrire. Voici quoi dire, et comment proposer la suite.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/se-faire-des-amis-apres-30-ans/, Se faire de vrais amis après 30 ans est tout à fait possible. Il faut plus d'intention et moins d'attente. Voici ce qui marche vraiment.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/garder-ses-amis-adulte/, Garder ses amis adulte tient moins aux grands moments qu'au contact régulier. La plupart des amitiés se perdent parce que personne ne propose la suite.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/pourquoi-les-amities-se-perdent/, La plupart des amitiés adultes ne se terminent pas. Elles s'éloignent doucement, sans dispute ni décision. Voici pourquoi, et comment les rattraper à temps.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/amitie-a-sens-unique/, Vous êtes la seule personne qui écrit, propose et relance ? Voici comment reconnaître une amitié à sens unique, et décider quoi en faire.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/rencontrer-du-monde-dans-une-nouvelle-ville/, Rencontrer du monde dans une nouvelle ville, ce n'est pas trouver l'événement parfait. C'est devenir un visage familier quelque part. Voici comment.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/ecrire-apres-une-rencontre/, La plupart des amitiés ne ratent pas à la première rencontre. Elles ratent dans le silence d'après. Voici quoi écrire, quand, et comment proposer la suite.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/transformer-une-connaissance-en-ami/, On connaît tous des gens qu'on apprécie sans les appeler des amis. L'écart n'est pas une question d'alchimie. C'est un pas que personne n'ose faire. Voici lequel.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/se-faire-des-amis-en-teletravail/, Le télétravail supprime la source la plus fiable de contact répété : le bureau. La solution n'est pas de le recréer, mais de construire autre chose. Voici quoi.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/se-faire-des-amis-expatrie/, Se faire des amis en expatriation est un vrai défi : pas de réseau, d'autres codes, une horloge qui tourne. Voici ce qui fonctionne vraiment à l'étranger.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/se-faire-des-amis-jeune-parent/, La jeune parentalité est une des périodes les plus isolantes de la vie adulte. Pas par manque de gens : par manque d'énergie et de structure. Voici comment faire.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/rester-amis-apres-un-bebe/, Quand votre ami a un bébé, son temps libre disparaît du jour au lendemain. Pour garder le lien, il faut changer les règles. Voici comment.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/garder-ses-amities-de-bureau-en-teletravail/, En passant en télétravail, on perd les moments de couloir qui faisaient vivre les amitiés de bureau. Voici comment remplacer cette structure avant qu'elles s'éteignent.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/devenir-un-meilleur-ami/, Devenir un meilleur ami, ce n'est pas les grands gestes. C'est la fiabilité ordinaire : faire attention, tenir parole, écrire en premier. Voici comment.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/savoir-si-quelquun-veut-devenir-votre-ami/, Est-ce de la politesse ou une vraie envie d'amitié ? Les signaux sont presque toujours là. Voici ce qu'il faut regarder, et quoi faire du doute.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/quand-une-amitie-se-termine/, Les ruptures d'amitié peuvent faire aussi mal que les ruptures amoureuses, et personne n'en parle. Voici comment traverser la fin, et ce qui peut rouvrir.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/se-faire-des-amis-apres-une-amitie-perdue/, Perdre un ami proche laisse un vide réel. Reconstruire est possible, mais différemment. Voici comment repartir, en portant la perte sans qu'elle décide de tout.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/pourquoi-les-hommes-perdent-leurs-amis/, La plupart des hommes ne perdent pas leurs amis d'un coup. Ils les perdent lentement, quand les contextes partagés disparaissent. Voici le mécanisme, et la sortie.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/comment-les-hommes-reconstruisent-leurs-amities/, Pas besoin de grande conversation ni de stage d'émotions. Il faut une activité récurrente, une invitation, et un message d'après. Voici la méthode.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/presenter-ses-amis-entre-eux/, Présenter deux personnes qu'on apprécie est un des gestes d'amitié les plus sous-estimés. Bien fait, il enrichit tout le monde. Voici la méthode douce.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/quest-ce-que-le-coaching-en-amitie/, Le coaching en amitié n'est pas une thérapie. C'est une stratégie pas à pas pour votre vie sociale, comme un coach sportif pour vos amitiés.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/fr/guides/citations-sur-lamitie/, Des citations sur l'amitié qui ne cherchent pas à inspirer, mais à clarifier : ce que l'amitié demande vraiment aux adultes, et ce qu'elle rend.

- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/why-making-friends-as-an-adult-is-hard/, Adulthood didn't kill your social skills. It destroyed your infrastructure. Here is why adult friendship requires deliberate design, not just more willpower.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-keep-friends-as-an-adult/, Adult friendships don't end in fights. They end in drift. Here is how to maintain them through consistent small acts, not grand gestures or perfect timing.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-meet-people-in-a-new-city/, Meeting people in a new city runs on repetition, not luck. Here is how to create the repeated contact that turns early acquaintances into real friendships.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-be-a-better-friend/, Being a better friend is less about grand gestures and more about reliable small ones: following up, showing up, and being easy to reach when it matters.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-after-30/, After 30, friendship doesn't stop being possible. It stops being automatic. Here is how to build it deliberately without pretending adult life works like college.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/why-male-friendships-fade/, Male friendships don't end in a fight. They fade through distance, cancelled plans, and the slow disappearance of shared contexts. Here's what the research shows.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-men-can-rebuild-friendships/, You don't need a deep conversation or a vulnerability retreat. You need a recurring activity, one invitation, and a follow-up. Here's how to rebuild.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/friendship-quotes/, Eight observations on adult friendship by Carole Stromboni, friendship coach: on following up, loneliness, repetition, and what actually builds closeness.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-introduce-friends-to-each-other/, A good introduction is about preparation, not spontaneity. Here is who to ask first, what to say, and when to step back and let the friendship form on its own.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/when-a-friendship-ends/, Friendship breakups can hurt as much as romantic ones, but nobody talks about them. Here is how to navigate the grief, accept the closure, and heal.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-stay-friends-after-a-baby/, When your friend has a baby, their free time drops to zero overnight. If you want to keep the bond, you have to change the rules. Here is how.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/one-sided-friendship/, Are you the only one making plans, sending texts, and checking in? Here is how to spot a one-sided friendship and decide what to do about it.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-when-you-work-from-home/, Remote work removes the easiest structure for repeated contact in adult life. Here is how to build a real social life when home is also your office.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-follow-up-after-meeting-someone/, Afraid to text someone you just met in case you seem too eager? Research shows people like us more than we think. Here is how to follow up without the stress.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/why-do-friendships-fade/, Friendships rarely end in a big fight. They drift away when nobody makes the next plan. Here is why it happens and how to catch it before it is too late.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-turn-acquaintances-into-friends/, How do you cross from someone you know to someone you trust? It takes more than hello. Here is what actually moves an acquaintance into a real friend.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-as-an-expat/, Making friends as an expat is a real challenge: no existing network, different cultural scripts, a ticking clock. Here is what actually works abroad.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-know-if-someone-wants-to-be-your-friend/, Reading the signals of mutual interest is one of the hardest parts of adult friendship. A practical guide to recognizing when a connection has real potential.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/what-is-friendship-coaching/, Friendship coaching isn't therapy. It is a step-by-step strategy for your social life, like a personal trainer for your friendships.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-as-a-new-parent/, New parenthood is isolating, and polite coffee dates aren't the answer. Here is how to find other parents who get it and build real connections fast.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-keep-work-friendships-after-going-remote/, When you go remote, you lose the hallway moments that kept work friendships alive. Here is how to replace that structure before the friendships quietly fade.
- https://thefriendshippractice.com/guides/how-to-make-friends-after-a-friendship-ends/, Losing a close friend leaves a real void. Clearing out a broken connection makes room for something healthier. Here is how to rebuild with confidence.

## Notes
- The coaching offer is practical support, not therapy or crisis care.
- Transactional pages such as confirmation and intake pages are intentionally excluded from search indexing.
- All quotes attributed to Carole Stromboni / The Friendship Practice.
